tools.name: delete_item
tools.title: Delete Item
tools.description: Delete an item

tools.name: get_board_items_page
tools.title: Get Board Items Page
tools.description: Get all items from a monday.com board with pagination support and optional column values. Returns structured JSON with item details, creation/update timestamps, and pagination info. Use the 'nextCursor' parameter from the response to get the next page of results when 'has_more' is true.[REQUIRED PRECONDITION]: Before using this tool, if new columns were added to the board or if you are not familiar with the board's structure (column IDs, column types, status labels, etc.), first use get_board_info to understand the board metadata. This is essential for constructing proper filters and knowing which columns are available.

boardId.description: The id of the board to get items from

itemIds.description: The ids of the items to get. The count of items should be less than 100.

searchTerm.description:
The search term to use for the search.
    - Use this when: the user provides a vague, incomplete, or approximate search term (e.g., “marketing campaign”, “John’s task”, “budget-related”), and there isn’t a clear exact compare value for a specific field.
    - Do not use this when: the user specifies an exact value that maps directly to a column comparison (e.g., name contains "marketing campaign", status = "Done", priority = "High", owner = "Daniel"). In these cases, prefer structured compare filters.

limit.description: The number of items to get

cursor.description: The cursor to get the next page of items, use the nextCursor from the previous response. If the nextCursor was null, it means there are no more items to get

includeColumns.description:
Whether to include column values in the response.
PERFORMANCE OPTIMIZATION: Only set this to true when you actually need the column data. Excluding columns significantly reduces token usage and improves response latency. If you only need to count items, get item IDs/names, or check if items exist, keep this false.

includeSubItems.description: Whether to include sub items in the response. PERFORMANCE OPTIMIZATION: Only set this to true when you actually need the sub items data.

subItemLimit.description: The number of sub items to get per item. This is only used when includeSubItems is true.

columnId.description: The id of the column to filter by

compareAttribute.description: The attribute to compare the value to. This is OPTIONAL property.

compareValue.description: The value to compare the attribute to. This can be a string or index value depending on the column type.

operator.description: The operator to use for the filter

filters.description: The configuration of filters to apply on the items. Before sending the filters, use get_board_info tool to check "filteringGuidelines" key for filtering by the column.

filtersOperator.description: The operator to use for the filters

columnIds.description: The ids of the item columns and subitem columns to get, can be used to reduce the response size when user asks for specific columns. Works only when includeColumns is true. If not provided, all columns will be returned

columnId.description: The id of the column to order by

orderBy.description: The columns to order by, will control the order of the items in the response

tools.name: create_item
tools.title: Create Item
tools.description: Create a new item with provided values, create a subitem under a parent item, or duplicate an existing item and update it with new values. Use parentItemId when creating a subitem under an existing item. Use duplicateFromItemId when copying an existing item with modifications.[REQUIRED PRECONDITION]: Before using this tool, if new columns were added to the board or if you are not familiar with the board's structure (column IDs, column types, status labels, etc.), first use get_board_info to understand the board metadata. This is essential for constructing proper column values and knowing which columns are available.

boardId.description: The id of the board to which the new item will be added

name.description: The name of the new item to be created, must be relevant to the user's request

groupId.description: The id of the group id to which the new item will be added, if its not clearly specified, leave empty

columnValues.description: A string containing the new column values for the item following this structure: {\"column_id\": \"value\",... you can change multiple columns at once, note that for status column you must use nested value with 'label' as a key and for date column use 'date' as key} - example: "{\"text_column_id\":\"New text\", \"status_column_id\":{\"label\":\"Done\"}, \"date_column_id\":{\"date\":\"2023-05-25\"},\"dropdown_id\":\"value\", \"phone_id\":\"123-456-7890\", \"email_id\":\"test@example.com\"}"

parentItemId.description: The id of the parent item under which the new subitem will be created

duplicateFromItemId.description: The id of existing item to duplicate and update with new values (only provide when duplicating)

tools.name: create_update
tools.title: Create Update
tools.description: Create a new update (comment/post) on a monday.com item. Updates can be used to add comments, notes, or discussions to items. You can optionally mention users, teams, or boards in the update. You can also reply to an existing update by using the parentId parameter.

itemId.description: The id of the item to which the update will be added

body.description: The update text to be created. Do not use @ to mention users, use the mentionsList field instead. use html tags to format the text, dont use markdown.

mentionsList.description: Optional JSON array of mentions in the format: [{"id": "123", "type": "User"}, {"id": "456", "type": "Team"}]. Valid types are: User, Team, Board, Project

parentId.description: The ID of the update to reply to. Use this parameter when you want to reply on an existing update leave it empty if you want to create a new update

tools.name: get_updates
tools.title: Get Updates
tools.description: Get updates (comments/posts) from a monday.com item or board. Specify objectId and objectType (Item or Board) to retrieve updates. Returns update text, creator info, timestamps, and optionally replies and assets.

objectId.description: The ID of the item or board to get updates from

objectType.description: Type of object for which objectId was provided

limit.description: Number of updates per page (default: 25, max: 100)

includeReplies.description: Include update replies in the response

includeAssets.description: Include file attachments in the response

tools.name: create_update_in_monday
tools.title: Create Update in Monday, after calling this tool you should
tools.description: Create a new update (comment/post) on a monday.com item. Updates can be used to add comments, notes, or discussions to items. You can optionally mention users, teams, or boards in the update. After calling this tool you should call the full board data tool to get data, and immediately after that call the show table tool to show the data from that tool. IMPORTANT: You MUST use the COMPLETE data from the full board data tool - do NOT cut, truncate, or omit any data. Pass the entire dataset to the show table tool.

itemId.description: The id of the item to which the update will be added

body.description: The update text to be created. Do not use @ to mention users, use the mentionsList field instead.

mentionsList.description: Optional JSON array of mentions in the format: [{"id": "123", "type": "User"}, {"id": "456", "type": "Team"}]. Valid types are: User, Team, Board, Project

annotations.title: Create Update in Monday, after calling this tool you should

tools.name: get_board_schema
tools.title: Get Board Schema
tools.description: Get board schema (columns and groups) by board id

boardId.description: The id of the board to get the schema of

tools.name: get_board_activity
tools.title: Get Board Activity
tools.description: Get board activity logs for a specified time range (defaults to last 30 days)

boardId.description: The id of the board to get activity for

fromDate.description: Start date for activity range (ISO8601DateTime format). Defaults to 30 days ago

toDate.description: End date for activity range (ISO8601DateTime format). Defaults to now

tools.name: get_board_info
tools.title: Get Board Info
tools.description: Get comprehensive board information including metadata, structure, owners, and configuration

boardId.description: The id of the board to get information for

tools.name: get_full_board_data
tools.title: Get Full Board Data
tools.description: INTERNAL USE ONLY - DO NOT CALL THIS TOOL DIRECTLY. This tool is exclusively triggered by UI components and should never be invoked directly by the agent.

boardId.description: The ID of the board to fetch complete data for

columnId.description: The id of the column to filter by

compareAttribute.description: The attribute to compare the value to. This is OPTIONAL property.

compareValue.description: The value to compare the attribute to. This can be a string or index value depending on the column type.

operator.description: The operator to use for the filter

filters.description: The configuration of filters to apply on the items. Before sending the filters, use get_board_info tool to check "filteringGuidelines" key for filtering by the column.

filtersOperator.description: The operator to use for the filters

tools.name: list_users_and_teams
tools.title: List Users and Teams
tools.description:
Tool to fetch users and/or teams data. 

      MANDATORY BEST PRACTICES:
      1. ALWAYS use specific IDs or names when available
      2. If no ids available, use name search if possible (USERS ONLY)
      3. Use 'getMe: true' to get current user information
      4. AVOID broad queries (no parameters) - use only as last resort

      REQUIRED PARAMETER PRIORITY (use in this order):
      1. getMe - STANDALONE
      2. userIds
      3. name - STANDALONE (USERS ONLY, NOT for teams)
      4. teamIds + teamsOnly
      5. No parameters - LAST RESORT

      CRITICAL USAGE RULES:
      • userIds + teamIds requires explicit includeTeams: true flag
      • includeTeams: true fetches both users and teams, do not use this to fetch a specific user's teams rather fetch that user by id and you will get their team memberships.
      • name parameter is for USER search ONLY - it cannot be used to search for teams. Use teamIds to fetch specific teams.

userIds.description: Specific user IDs to fetch.[IMPORTANT] ALWAYS use when you have user IDs in context. PREFER over general search. RETURNS: user profiles including team memberships

teamIds.description:
Specific team IDs to fetch.[IMPORTANT] ALWAYS use when you have team IDs in context, NEVER fetch all teams if specific IDs are available.
      RETURNS: Team details with owners and optional member data.

name.description:
Name-based USER search ONLY. STANDALONE parameter - cannot be combined with others. PREFERRED method for finding users when you know names. Performs fuzzy matching.
      CRITICAL: This parameter searches for USERS ONLY, NOT teams. To search for teams, use teamIds parameter instead.

getMe.description:
[TOP PRIORITY] Use ALWAYS when requesting current user information. Examples of when it should be used: ["get my user" or "get my teams"].
      This parameter CONFLICTS with all others.

includeTeams.description: [AVOID] This fetches all teams in the account. To fetch a specific user's teams just fetch that user by id and you will get their team memberships.

teamsOnly.description: Fetch only teams, no users returned. Combine with includeTeamMembers for member details.

includeTeamMembers.description: Set to true only when you need additional member details for teams other than names and ids.

tools.name: change_item_column_values
tools.title: Change Item Column Values
tools.description: Change the column values of an item in a monday.com board

boardId.description: The ID of the board that contains the item to be updated

itemId.description: The ID of the item to be updated

columnValues.description: A string containing the new column values for the item following this structure: {\"column_id\": \"value\",... you can change multiple columns at once, note that for status column you must use nested value with 'label' as a key and for date column use 'date' as key} - example: "{\"text_column_id\":\"New text\", \"status_column_id\":{\"label\":\"Done\"}, \"date_column_id\":{\"date\":\"2023-05-25\"}, \"phone_id\":\"123-456-7890\", \"email_id\":\"test@example.com\"}"

tools.name: move_item_to_group
tools.title: Move Item to Group
tools.description: Move an item to a group in a monday.com board

itemId.description: The id of the item to which the update will be added

groupId.description: The id of the group to which the item will be moved

tools.name: create_board
tools.title: Create Board
tools.description: Create a monday.com board

boardName.description: The name of the board to create

boardKind.description: The kind of board to create

boardDescription.description: The description of the board to create

workspaceId.description: The ID of the workspace to create the board in

tools.name: create_form
tools.title: Create Form
tools.description: Create a monday.com form. This will create a new form as well as a new board for which the form’s responses will be stored. The returned board_id is the ID of the board that was created while the returned formToken can be used for all future queries and mutations to continue editing the form.

destination_workspace_id.description: The workspace in which the form will be created in.

destination_folder_id.description: The folder in which the form will be created under.

destination_folder_name.description: The name of the folder in which the form will be created in.

board_kind.description: The board kind to create for the board in which the form will create items in.

destination_name.description: The name of the board that will be created to store the form responses in.

board_owner_ids.description: Array of user IDs who will have owner permissions on the board in which the form will create items in.

board_owner_team_ids.description: Array of team IDs whose members will have owner permissions on the board in which the form will create items in.

board_subscriber_ids.description: Array of user IDs who will receive notifications about board activity for the board in which the form will create items in.

board_subscriber_teams_ids.description: Array of team IDs whose members will receive notifications about board activity for the board in which the form will create items in.

tools.name: update_form
tools.title: Update Form
tools.description:
Update a monday.com form. Handles the following form update actions that can only be done one at a time using the correct "action" input: 
    - update form's feature settings with the action "updateFeatures",
    - update form's appearance settings with the action "updateAppearance",
    - update form's accessibility settings with the action "updateAccessibility",
    - update form's title with the action "updateFormHeader",
    - update form's description with the action "updateFormHeader",
    - update form's question order with the action "updateQuestionOrder",
    - create a new form tag with the action "createTag",
    - delete a form tag with the action "deleteTag",
    - update a form tag with the action "updateTag",
    - set or update the form's password with the action "setFormPassword"
    - shorten form's url with the action "shortenFormUrl"
    - deactivate form with the action "deactivateForm"
    - reactivate form with the action "activateForm"

formToken.description: The unique identifier token for the form. Required for all form-specific operations.

action.description: The type of update action to perform on the form. Can be one of the following: activate, deactivate, shortenFormUrl, setFormPassword, createTag, deleteTag, updateTag, updateForm, updateAppearance, updateAccessibility, updateFeatures, updateQuestionOrder, updateFormHeader.

formPassword.description: Set a password on a form to restrict access. This will enable password protection for the form. Required for the action "setFormPassword" in the update form tool.

id.description: The unique identifier for the tag. This will get auto generated when creating a tag and can’t be updated. This is required when updating or deleting a tag.

name.description: The name of the tag. This can only be created, not updated. This is required when creating a tag.

value.description: The value of the tag. This value is required when creating or updating a tag.

columnId.description: The ID of the column this tag is associated with. This will get auto generated when creating a tag and can’t be updated.

tag.description: The tag data to create, update or delete. If deleting a tag, only provide the id of the tag to delete. If creating a tag, provide the name and value, the id and columnId are auto generated. If updating a tag, provide the id and new value, name and columnId are not changeable.

value.description: String containing the background value. The value will depend on the background type. If the background type is color, the value will be a hex color code. If the background type is image, the value will be an image URL.

background.description: Object containing background appearance configuration for the form.

hideBranding.description: Boolean hiding monday branding from the form display.

format.description: String specifying the form display format. Can be a step by step form or a classic one page form.

alignment.description: String controlling text and content alignment.

layout.description: Object containing form structure and presentation settings.

size.description: String specifying logo size ("small", "medium", "large") for the logo that appears on the header of the form.

logo.description: Object containing logo display configuration for form branding.

primaryColor.description: Hex color code for the primary theme color used throughout the form.

showProgressBar.description: Boolean displaying a progress indicator showing form completion progress bar.

text.description: Custom text displayed on the form submission button.

submitButton.description: Object containing submit button styling and text configuration.

font.description: String specifying the font family used throughout the form.

color.description: Hex color code for the text color in the form.

size.description: String or number specifying the base font size for form text.

text.description: Object containing typography and text styling configuration.

appearance.description: The appearance data to update. Acts as a patch object, meaning that only the fields that are provided will be updated. Required if the update action is updateAppearance.

language.description: Language code for form localization and interface text (e.g., "en", "es", "fr").

logoAltText.description: Alternative text description for the logo image for accessibility.

accessibility.description: The accessibility data to update. Acts as a patch object, meaning that only the fields that are provided will be updated. Required if the update action is updateAccessibility.

allowEditSubmission.description: Boolean allowing users to modify their submitted responses after submission.

allowResubmit.description: Boolean allowing users to submit multiple responses to the same form.

allowViewSubmission.description: Boolean allowing users to view their submitted responses.

description.description: Text shown to users after they complete the form.

enabled.description: Boolean enabling automatic redirect after form completion to a specified URL.

redirectUrl.description: The URL where users will be redirected after successfully submitting the form.

redirectAfterSubmission.description: Object containing redirect configuration after form submission.

showSuccessImage.description: Boolean displaying a success image after form completion.

title.description: Text displayed as the title after successful form submission.

afterSubmissionView.description: Object containing settings for the post-submission user experience.

enabled.description: Boolean enabling automatic form closure at a specified date and time.

date.description: ISO timestamp when the form will automatically stop accepting responses.

closeDate.description: Object containing automatic form closure configuration.

enabled.description: Boolean allowing users to save incomplete responses as drafts.

draftSubmission.description: Object containing draft saving configuration allowing users to save progress.

itemGroupId.description: The board group ID where new items from form responses will be created.

includeNameQuestion.description: Boolean adding a name question to the form. This is a special question type that represents the name column from the associated monday board

includeUpdateQuestion.description: Boolean adding an update/comment field to the form. This is a special question type that represents the updates from the associated item of the submission on the monday board.

syncQuestionAndColumnsTitles.description: Boolean synchronizing form question titles with board column names. When true, the form question titles will be synchronized with the board column names.

monday.description: Object containing board settings for response handling.

enabled.description: Boolean disabling password protection. Can only be updated to false. In order to enable password protection use the setFormPassword action instead.

password.description: Object containing password protection configuration for the form.

enabled.description: Boolean showing a welcome/introduction screen before the form begins.

title.description: Text displayed as the title on the welcome screen.

description.description: Text providing context or instructions on the welcome screen.

text.description: Custom text for the button that begins the form experience.

startButton.description: Object containing start button configuration for the welcome screen.

preSubmissionView.description: Object containing welcome screen configuration displayed before the form.

reCaptchaChallenge.description: Boolean enabling reCAPTCHA verification to prevent spam submissions.

enabled.description: Boolean requiring users to be logged in before submitting responses.

redirectToLogin.description: Boolean automatically redirecting unauthenticated users to the login page.

requireLogin.description: Object containing login requirement settings for form access.

enabled.description: Boolean enabling response count limits for the form.

limit.description: Integer specifying the maximum number of responses allowed.

responseLimit.description: Object containing response limitation settings to control submission volume.

features.description: The features data to update. Acts as a patch object, meaning that only the fields that are provided will be updated. Required if the update action is updateFeatures.

title.description: The title text for the form. Must be at least 1 character long. Can only be updated if the update action is updateFormHeader.

description.description: Optional description text providing context about the form purpose. Can only be updated if the update action is updateFormHeader.

id.description: The unique identifier for the question. Used to target specific questions within a form. Required when deleting or updating a question.

questions.description: Ordered array of dehydrated questions, object only including each question ID, for reordering. Must include all existing question IDs. Required if the update action is updateQuestionOrder.

form.description: The form data to update. Required if updating the appearance, accessibility, features, question order, or form header.

tools.name: get_form
tools.title: Get Form
tools.description: Get a monday.com form by its form token. Form tokens can be extracted from the form’s url. Given a form url, such as https://forms.monday.com/forms/abc123def456ghi789?r=use1, the token is the alphanumeric string that appears right after /forms/ and before the ?. In the example, the token is abc123def456ghi789.

formToken.description: The unique identifier token for the form. Required for all form-specific operations.

tools.name: form_questions_editor
tools.title: Form Questions Editor
tools.description: Create, update, or delete a question in a monday.com form

action.description: The type of operation to perform on the question. Can delete, update, or create. When updating or deleting a question, the questionId is required. When creating or updating a question, the question object is required. When updating, the question is a patch object, meaning that only the fields that are provided will be updated.

formToken.description: The unique identifier token for the form. Required for all form-specific operations.

questionId.description: The unique identifier for the question. Used to target specific questions within a form.

type.description: The question type determining input behavior and validation (e.g., "text", "email", "single_select", "multi_select").

title.description: The question text displayed to respondents. Must be at least 1 character long and clearly indicate the expected response.

description.description: Optional explanatory text providing additional context, instructions, or examples for the question.

visible.description: Boolean controlling question visibility to respondents. Hidden questions remain in form structure but are not displayed.

required.description: Boolean indicating if the question must be answered before form submission.

label.description: The display text for individual option choices in select-type questions.

options.description: Array of option objects for choice-based questions (single_select, multi_select). Required when creating select type questions. Can only be provided when creating a question, not yet supported for updating a question.

checkedByDefault.description: Boolean/checkbox questions only: Whether the checkbox should be checked by default when the form loads.

defaultCurrentDate.description: Date based questions only: Automatically set the current date as the default value when the form loads.

display.description: Single/Multi Select questions only: Controls how the selection options are visually presented to users.

includeTime.description: Date questions only: Whether to include time selection (hours and minutes) in addition to the date picker. When false, only date selection is available.

locationAutofilled.description: Location questions only: Automatically detect and fill the user's current location using browser geolocation services, requiring user permission.

optionsOrder.description: Single/Multi Select questions only: Determines the ordering of selection options.

prefixAutofilled.description: Phone questions only: Automatically detect and fill the phone country prefix based on the user's geographic location or browser settings.

enabled.description: Whether a predefined phone prefix is enabled for phone number questions. When true, the specified prefix will be pre-selected.

prefix.description: The predefined phone country prefix to use as country code in capital letters (e.g., "US", "UK", "IL"). Only used when enabled is true.

prefixPredefined.description: Phone questions only: Configuration for setting a specific predefined phone country prefix that will be pre-selected for users.

skipValidation.description: Link/URL questions only: Whether to skip URL format validation, allowing any text input.

enabled.description: Whether prefill functionality is enabled for this question. When true, the question will attempt to auto-populate values from the specified source.

lookup.description: The specific field or parameter name to lookup from the prefill source. For account sources, this would be a user property like "name" or "email". For query parameters, this would be the parameter name that would be set in the URL.

source.description: The data source to use for prefilling the question value. Check the PrefillSources for available options.

prefill.description: Configuration for automatically populating question values from various data sources such as user account information or URL query parameters.

question.description: The question object containing all properties for creation or update. When creating a question, the title is required.

tools.name: create_column
tools.title: Create Column
tools.description: Create a new column in a monday.com board

boardId.description: The id of the board to which the new column will be added

columnType.description: The type of the column to be created

columnTitle.description: The title of the column to be created

columnDescription.description: The description of the column to be created

columnSettings.description: Column-specific configuration settings as a JSON string. Use the get_column_type_info tool to fetch the JSON schema for the given column type.

tools.name: create_group
tools.title: Create Group
tools.description: Create a new group in a monday.com board. Groups are sections that organize related items. Use when users want to add structure, categorize items, or create workflow phases. Groups can be positioned relative to existing groups and assigned predefined colors. Items will always be created in the top group and so the top group should be the most relevant one for new item creation

boardId.description: The ID of the board to create the group in

groupName.description: The name of the new group (maximum 255 characters)

groupColor.description: The color for the group. Must be one of the predefined Monday.com group colors: #037f4c, #00c875, #9cd326, #cab641, #ffcb00, #784bd1, #9d50dd, #007eb5, #579bfc, #66ccff, #bb3354, #df2f4a, #ff007f, #ff5ac4, #ff642e, #fdab3d, #7f5347, #c4c4c4, #757575

relativeTo.description: The ID of the group to position this new group relative to

positionRelativeMethod.description: Whether to position the new group before or after the relativeTo group

tools.name: delete_column
tools.title: Delete Column
tools.description: Delete a column from a monday.com board

boardId.description: The id of the board to which the new column will be added

columnId.description: The id of the column to be deleted

tools.name: get_column_type_info
tools.title: Get Column Type Info
tools.description: Retrieves comprehensive information about a specific column type, including JSON schema definition and other metadata. Use this before creating columns with the create_column tool to understand the structure, validation rules, and available properties for column settings.

columnType.description: The column type to retrieve information for (e.g., "text", "status", "date", "numbers")

tools.name: create_custom_activity
tools.title: Create Custom Activity
tools.description: Create a new custom activity in the E&A app

color.description: The color of the custom activity

icon_id.description: The icon ID of the custom activity

name.description: The name of the custom activity

tools.name: create_notification
tools.title: Create Notification
tools.description: Send a notification to a user via the bell icon and optionally by email. Use target_type "Post" for updates/replies or "Project" for items/boards.

user_id.description: The user ID to send the notification to

target_id.description: The target ID (update/reply ID for Post type, item/board ID for Project type)

target_type.description: The target type (Post for update/reply, Project for item/board)

tools.name: create_timeline_item
tools.title: Create Timeline Item
tools.description: Create a new timeline item in the E&A app

item_id.description: The ID of the item to create the new timeline item on

custom_activity_id.description: The ID of the custom activity for the timeline item

title.description: The title of the new timeline item

summary.description: The summary of the new timeline item (max 255 characters)

content.description: The content of the new timeline item

timestamp.description: The creation time of the new timeline item in ISO8601 format (e.g., 2024-06-06T18:00:30Z)

start_timestamp.description: The start time of the timeline item in ISO8601 format

end_timestamp.description: The end time of the timeline item in ISO8601 format

location.description: The location to add to the new timeline item

phone.description: The phone number to add to the new timeline item

url.description: The URL to add to the new timeline item

tools.name: fetch_custom_activity
tools.title: Fetch Custom Activities
tools.description: Get custom activities from the E&A app

tools.name: read_docs
tools.title: Read Documents
tools.description:
Get a collection of monday.com documents with their content as markdown. 

PAGINATION: 
- Default limit is 25 documents per page
- Use 'page' parameter to get additional pages (starts at 1)
- Check response for 'has_more_pages' to know if you should continue paginating
- If user asks for "all documents" and you get exactly 25 results, continue with page 2, 3, etc.

FILTERING: Provide a type value and array of ids:
- type: 'ids' for specific document IDs
- type: 'object_ids' for specific document object IDs  
- type: 'workspace_ids' for all docs in specific workspaces
- ids: array of ID strings (at least 1 required)

Examples:
- { type: 'ids', ids: ['123', '456'] }
- { type: 'object_ids', ids: ['123'] }
- { type: 'workspace_ids', ids: ['ws_101'] }

USAGE PATTERNS:
- For specific documents: use type 'ids' or 'object_ids' (A monday doc has two unique identifiers)
- For workspace exploration: use type 'workspace_ids' with pagination
- For large searches: start with page 1, then paginate if has_more_pages=true

type.description: Query type of ids parameter that is used query by: ids, object_ids, or workspace_ids

ids.description: Array of ID values for this query type (at least 1 required)

limit.description: Number of docs per page (default: 25). Affects pagination - if you get exactly this many results, there may be more pages.

order_by.description: The order in which to retrieve your docs. The default shows created_at with the newest docs listed first. This argument will not be applied if you query docs by specific ids.

page.description: The page number to return (starts at 1). Use this to paginate through large result sets. Check response for has_more_pages indicator.

tools.name: workspace_info
tools.title: Get Workspace Information
tools.description: This tool returns the boards, docs and folders in a workspace and which folder they are in. It returns up to 100 of each object type, if you receive 100 assume there are additional objects of that type in the workspace.

workspace_id.description: The ID of the workspace to get information for

tools.name: list_workspaces
tools.title: List Workspaces
tools.description: List all workspaces available to the user. Returns up to 500 workspaces with their ID, name, and description.

searchTerm.description: Optional search term used to filter workspaces. [IMPORANT] Only alphanumeric characters are supported.

limit.description: Number of workspaces to return. Set to max (100) lower for smaller response size

page.description: Page number to return. Default is 1.

tools.name: create_doc
tools.title: Create Document

markdown.description: Markdown content that will be imported into the newly created document as blocks.

location.description: Location where the document should be created - either in a workspace or attached to an item

workspace_id.description: [REQUIRED - use only when location="workspace"] Workspace ID under which to create the new document

doc_kind.description: [OPTIONAL - use only when location="workspace"] Document kind (public/private/share). Defaults to public.

folder_id.description: [OPTIONAL - use only when location="workspace"] Optional folder ID to place the document inside a specific folder

item_id.description: [REQUIRED - use only when location="item"] Item ID to attach the new document to

column_id.description: [OPTIONAL - use only when location="item"] ID of an existing "doc" column on the board which contains the item. If not provided, the tool will create a new doc column automatically when creating a doc on an item.

tools.name: add_content_to_doc
tools.title: Add Content to Document

doc_id.description: The document ID (the id field returned by read_docs). Provide this OR object_id. Takes priority if both are provided.

object_id.description: The document object ID (the object_id field from read_docs, also visible in the document URL). Will be resolved to a doc_id. Provide this OR doc_id.

markdown.description: Markdown content to add to the document.

after_block_id.description: Block ID after which to insert the new content. If omitted, content is appended at the end. To insert at the beginning, pass the first block ID from read_docs. Block IDs can be obtained from read_docs or from a previous add_content_to_doc response.

tools.name: update_workspace
tools.title: Update Workspace
tools.description: Update an existing workspace in monday.com

id.description: The ID of the workspace to update

attributeAccountProductId.description: The target account product's ID to move the workspace to

attributeDescription.description: The description of the workspace to update

attributeKind.description: The kind of the workspace to update (open / closed / template)

attributeName.description: The name of the workspace to update

tools.name: update_folder
tools.title: Update Folder
tools.description: Update an existing folder in monday.com

folderId.description: The ID of the folder to update

name.description: The new name of the folder

color.description: The new color of the folder

fontWeight.description: The new font weight of the folder

customIcon.description: The new custom icon of the folder

parentFolderId.description: The ID of the new parent folder

workspaceId.description: The ID of the workspace containing the folder

accountProductId.description: The account product ID associated with the folder

position_object_id.description: The ID of the object to position the folder relative to. If this parameter is provided, position_object_type must be also provided.

position_object_type.description: The type of object to position the folder relative to. If this parameter is provided, position_object_id must be also provided.

position_is_after.description: Whether to position the folder after the object

tools.name: create_workspace
tools.title: Create Workspace
tools.description: Create a new workspace in monday.com

name.description: The name of the new workspace to be created

workspaceKind.description: The kind of workspace to create

description.description: The description of the new workspace

accountProductId.description: The account product ID associated with the workspace

tools.name: create_folder
tools.title: Create Folder
tools.description: Create a new folder in a monday.com workspace

workspaceId.description: The ID of the workspace where the folder will be created

name.description: The name of the folder to be created

fontWeight.description: The font weight of the folder

customIcon.description: The custom icon of the folder

parentFolderId.description: The ID of the parent folder

tools.name: move_object
tools.title: Move Object
tools.description: Move a folder, board, or overview in monday.com. Use `position` for relative placement based on another object, `parentFolderId` for folder changes, `workspaceId` for workspace moves, and `accountProductId` for account product changes.

objectType.description: The type of object to move

id.description: The ID of the object to move

position_object_id.description: The ID of the object to position the object relative to. If this parameter is provided, position_object_type must be also provided.

position_object_type.description: The type of object to position the object relative to. If this parameter is provided, position_object_id must be also provided.

position_is_after.description: Whether to position the object after the object

parentFolderId.description: The ID of the new parent folder. Required if moving to a different folder.

workspaceId.description: The ID of the workspace containing the object. Required if moving to a different workspace.

accountProductId.description: The ID of the account product containing the object. Required if moving to a different account product.

tools.name: create_dashboard
tools.title: Create Dashboard
tools.description:
Use this tool to create a new monday.com dashboard that aggregates data from one or more boards. 
    Dashboards provide visual representations of board data through widgets and charts.
    
    Use this tool when users want to:
    - Create a dashboard to visualize board data
    - Aggregate information from multiple boards
    - Set up a data visualization container for widgets

workspace_id.description: ID of the workspace that will own the dashboard

board_ids.description: List of board IDs as strings (min 1 element)

board_folder_id.description: Optional folder ID within workspace to place this dashboard (if not provided, dashboard will be placed in workspace root)

tools.name: all_widgets_schema
tools.title: Get All Widget Schemas
tools.description:
Fetch complete JSON Schema 7 definitions for all available widget types in monday.com.
    
    This tool is essential before creating widgets as it provides:
    - Complete schema definitions for all supported widgets
    - Required and optional fields for each widget type
    - Data type specifications and validation rules
    - Detailed descriptions of widget capabilities
    
    Use this tool when you need to:
    - Understand widget configuration requirements before creating widgets
    - Validate widget settings against official schemas
    - Plan widget implementations with proper data structures
    
    The response includes JSON Schema 7 definitions that describe exactly what settings each widget type accepts.

tools.name: create_widget
tools.title: Create Widget
tools.description:
Create a new widget in a dashboard or board view with specific configuration settings.
    
    This tool creates data visualization widgets that display information from monday.com boards:
    **Parent Containers:**
    - **DASHBOARD**: Place widget in a dashboard (most common use case)
    - **BOARD_VIEW**: Place widget in a specific board view
    
    **Critical Requirements:**
    1. **Schema Compliance**: Widget settings MUST conform to the JSON schema for the specific widget type
    2. **Use all_widgets_schema first**: Always fetch widget schemas before creating widgets
    3. **Validate settings**: Ensure all required fields are provided and data types match
    
    **Workflow:**
    1. Use 'all_widgets_schema' to get schema definitions
    2. Prepare widget settings according to the schema
    3. Use this tool to create the widget

parent_container_id.description: ID of the parent container (dashboard ID or board view ID)

parent_container_type.description: Type of parent container: DASHBOARD or BOARD_VIEW

widget_kind.description: Type of widget to create: i.e CHART, NUMBER, BATTERY

settings.description: Widget-specific settings as JSON object conforming to widget schema. Use all_widgets_schema tool to get the required schema for each widget type.

tools.name: board_insights
tools.title: Get Board Insights
tools.description: This tool allows you to calculate insights about board's data by filtering, grouping and aggregating columns. For example, you can get the total number of items in a board, the number of items in each status, the number of items in each column, etc. Use this tool when you need to get a summary of the board's data, for example, you want to know the total number of items in a board, the number of items in each status, the number of items in each column, etc.[REQUIRED PRECONDITION]: Before using this tool, if new columns were added to the board or if you are not familiar with the board's structure (column IDs, column types, status labels, etc.), first use get_board_info to understand the board metadata. This is essential for constructing proper filters and knowing which columns are available.[IMPORTANT]: For some columns, human-friendly label is returned inside 'LABEL_<column_id' field. E.g. for column with id 'status_123' the label is returned inside 'LABEL_status_123' field.

boardId.description: The id of the board to get insights for

function.description: The function of the aggregation. For simple column value leave undefined

columnId.description: The id of the column to aggregate

aggregations.description: The aggregations to get. Before sending the aggregations, use get_board_info tool to check "aggregationGuidelines" key for information. Transformative functions and plain columns (no function) must be in group by.

groupBy.description: The columns to group by. All columns in the group by must be in the aggregations as well without a function.

columnId.description: The id of the column to filter by

compareAttribute.description: The attribute to compare the value to. This is OPTIONAL property.

compareValue.description: The value to compare the attribute to. This can be a string or index value depending on the column type.

operator.description: The operator to use for the filter

filters.description: The configuration of filters to apply on the items. Before sending the filters, use get_board_info tool to check "filteringGuidelines" key for filtering by the column.

filtersOperator.description: The operator to use for the filters

columnId.description: The id of the column to order by

orderBy.description: The columns to order by, will control the order of the items in the response

tools.name: search
tools.title: Search
tools.description:
Search within monday.com platform. Can search for boards, documents, forms, folders.
For users and teams, use list_users_and_teams tool.
For workspaces, use list_workspaces tool.
For items and groups, use get_board_items_page tool.
For groups, use get_board_info tool.
IMPORTANT: ids returned by this tool are prefixed with the type of the object (e.g doc-123, board-456, folder-789). When passing the ids to other tools, you need to remove the prefix and just pass the number.

searchTerm.description: The search term to use for the search.

searchType.description: The type of search to perform.

limit.description: The number of items to get. The max and default value is 100.

page.description: The page number to get. The default value is 1.

workspaceIds.description: The ids of the workspaces to search in. [IMPORTANT] Only pass this param if user explicitly asked to search within specific workspaces.

tools.name: get_user_context
tools.title: Get User Context
tools.description:
Fetch current user information and their relevant items (boards, folders, workspaces, dashboards).
    
    Use this tool at the beginning of conversations to:
    - Get context about who the current user is (id, name, title)
    - Discover user's favorite boards, folders, workspaces, and dashboards
    - Get user's most relevant boards based on visit frequency and recency
    - Get user's most relevant people based on interaction frequency and recency
    - Reduce the need for search requests by knowing user's commonly accessed items

tools.name: update_assets_on_item
tools.title: Update Assets On Item
tools.description: Update a file or doc column value on an item using existing assets, docs, or links. Sets the column to the provided list of files, adding new ones and removing any not in the list.

columnId.description: The file or doc column's unique identifier

fileType.description: The type of file: "asset" for uploaded files (requires assetId), "doc" for monday docs (requires objectId), "link" for generic links, "google_drive", "dropbox", "box", "onedrive" for cloud storage links (all link types require linkToFile)

linkToFile.description: File link URL. Required for link, google_drive, dropbox, box, and onedrive file types

assetId.description: The asset's ID. Required when fileType is 'asset'

objectId.description: The doc's ID. Required when fileType is 'doc'

files.description: Array of file values to set on the column

tools.name: get_notetaker_meetings
tools.title: Get Notetaker Meetings
tools.description: Retrieve notetaker meetings with optional detailed fields. Use include_summary, include_topics, include_action_items, and include_transcript flags to control which details are returned. Supports filtering by ids, search term, and cursor-based pagination.

ids.description: Filter by specific meeting IDs. Use this to fetch one or more specific meetings in a single call.

limit.description: Maximum number of notetaker meetings to return per page (1-100).

cursor.description: Cursor for pagination. Use cursor from the previous page_info to fetch the next page.

search.description: Search notetaker meetings by title, participant name, or email.

include_summary.description: Whether to include the AI-generated summary for each meeting.

include_topics.description: Whether to include discussion topics and talking points for each meeting.

include_action_items.description: Whether to include action items for each meeting.

include_transcript.description: Whether to include the full transcript for each meeting. Transcripts can be very large.

tools.name: get_monday_dev_sprints_boards
tools.title: monday-dev: Get Sprints Boards
tools.description:
Discover monday-dev sprints boards and their associated tasks boards in your account.

## Purpose:
Identifies and returns monday-dev sprints board IDs and tasks board IDs that you need to use with other monday-dev tools. 
This tool scans your recently used boards (up to 100) to find valid monday-dev sprint management boards.

## What it Returns:
- Pairs of sprints boards and their corresponding tasks boards
- Board IDs, names, and workspace information for each pair
- The bidirectional relationship between each sprints board and its tasks board

## Note:
Searches recently used boards (up to 100). If none found, ask user to provide board IDs manually.

tools.name: get_sprints_metadata
tools.title: monday-dev: Get Sprints Metadata
tools.description:
Get comprehensive sprint metadata from a monday-dev sprints board including:

## Data Retrieved:
A table of sprints with the following information:
- Sprint ID
- Sprint Name
- Sprint timeline (planned from/to dates)
- Sprint completion status (completed/in-progress/planned)
- Sprint start date (actual)
- Sprint end date (actual)
- Sprint activation status
- Sprint summary document object ID

## Parameters:
- **limit**: Number of sprints to retrieve (default: 25, max: 100)

Requires the Main Sprints board ID of the monday-dev containing your sprints.

sprintsBoardId.description: The ID of the monday-dev board containing the sprints

limit.description: The number of sprints to retrieve (default: 25, max: 100)

tools.name: get_sprint_summary
tools.title: monday-dev: Get Sprint Summary
tools.description:
Get the complete summary and analysis of a sprint.

## Purpose:
Unlock deep insights into completed sprint performance. 

The sprint summary content including:
- **Scope Management**: Analysis of planned vs. unplanned tasks, scope creep
- **Velocity & Performance**: Individual velocity, task completion rates, workload distribution per team member
- **Task Distribution**: Breakdown of completed tasks by type (Feature, Bug, Tech Debt, Infrastructure, etc.)
- **AI Recommendations**: Action items, process improvements, retrospective focus areas

## Requirements:
- Sprint must be completed and must be created after 1/1/2025 

## Important Note:
When viewing the section "Completed by Assignee", you'll see user IDs in the format "@user-12345678". the 8 digits after the @is the user ID. To retrieve the actual owner names, use the list_users_and_teams tool with the user ID and set includeTeams=false for optimal performance.

sprintId.description: The ID of the sprint to get the summary for (e.g., "9123456789")
